description_long: This is a rectangular, two-story research institute building with a modern architectural design. The facade features large, continuous glass windows framed by metal, allowing ample natural light into the interior. The structure is primarily composed of concrete or brick with a flat, dark-colored roof, and a distinct section on one end that likely houses the main entrance. The side and rear elevations are simpler, with evenly spaced windows and minimal decorative elements. The building's exterior is finished in neutral tones, giving it a professional and institutional appearance. The overall design emphasizes functionality, transparency, and accessibility, suitable for an urban academic or research environment.
description: A modern, rectangular research institute with large glass windows, a flat roof, and a neutral-toned facade. The design is functional and professional, emphasizing transparency and accessibility.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main glass facade with two stories of large windows and a prominent entry section on the right.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the building's narrow end with two stacked windows and a plain brick wall.
description_view_2: Rear view featuring a long, unadorned wall with evenly spaced windows and minimal architectural detail.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with two stacked windows and a plain brick surface, mirroring the other end.
attribute_entryway_design: The main entryway is located on the right side of the front facade, distinguished by a vertical section with a darker finish. It features a large glass door integrated into a floor-to-ceiling glass panel, providing visibility and accessibility. The entry is at ground level with no visible steps, suggesting wheelchair accessibility, and is framed by metal or concrete elements. There are no decorative elements or signage present.
